subject: First Aid & Safety
title: The Birth of the Red Cross
description: Learn The Birth of the Red Cross in one minute.

hook.text: Henry Dunant saw 40,000 wounded soldiers abandoned on a battlefield in 1859. He couldn't look away.

buildup.text: After the Battle of Solferino, Dunant organized local volunteers to treat the wounded.

discovery.text: He wrote 'A Memory of Solferino,' proposing neutral relief societies in every country.

twist.text: His idea became the International Red Cross and the Geneva Conventions.

climax.text: Dunant won the first Nobel Peace Prize in 1901 for changing the rules of war forever.

punchline.text: One man's horror became the world's largest humanitarian network.

quiz.question: What inspired Henry Dunant to create the Red Cross?
options: Witnessing wounded soldiers abandoned at the Battle of Solferino
His experience as a military doctor
A request from the Swiss government
